overview: The iShares S&P/TSX 60 Index ETF (XIU.TO) tracks the S&P/TSX 60 Index, comprising 60 of the largest and most liquid Canadian stocks. As Canada's oldest and most traded ETF, XIU provides blue-chip exposure across financials, energy, materials, and other key Canadian sectors. With approximately CAD $12 billion in AUM, it is the largest equity ETF on the TSX. XIU's MER is 0.18%, and it is known for exceptional liquidity with tight bid-ask spreads, making it a staple for both retail and institutional investors.
bullCase: Canada's most liquid ETF with ~CAD $12 billion AUM and extremely tight bid-ask spreads.
Low MER of 0.18% for blue-chip Canadian large-cap exposure.
Solid dividend yield from Canada's top 60 companies, including major banks and energy producers.
bearCase: Only 60 stocks — less diversified than broad-market alternatives like XIC (~240 stocks).
Heavy concentration in financials and energy sectors, tied to commodity and interest rate cycles.
Large-cap only — no exposure to mid or small-cap growth opportunities.
analystSummary: XIU remains the benchmark Canadian equity ETF. Its 0.18% MER, ~$12B AUM, and unmatched liquidity make it the default choice for S&P/TSX 60 exposure, though investors wanting broader coverage may prefer XIC.
seoDescription: Explore whether to buy XIU with our comprehensive analysis of the iShares S&P/TSX 60 Index ETF — Canada's largest equity ETF at 0.18% MER.
